canto_title: The Final Interrogation and the Proclamation of Faith

scenes.summary: Beatrice directs the blessed souls to focus their hearts on the immense grace they receive from the divine banquet.
The souls move in a complex, rotating dance resembling golden ornaments or spinning comets.
A uniquely bright and joyful flame emerges from the light, dazzling Dante with its clarity.

scenes.scene_name: Dante’s Prayer and the Flame’s Turn
scenes.summary: Dante asks the flame to separate him from the distance of Beatrice, prompting the flame to turn toward him.
The flame acknowledges Beatrice and explains that it is testing Dante's faith as part of the divine plan.
Dante compares himself to a student ready with arguments, prepared to answer the coming theological questions.
The flame poses its fundamental inquiry to Dante: 'What is faith?'
Dante prays to the Holy Spirit to grant him the grace to express his thoughts with precision.
Dante defines faith as the substance of things hoped for and the evidence of things unseen.
The flame probes how faith functions as both a substance and an argument based on evidence.
The flame warns that if faith were merely a matter of learned doctrine, it would be subject to the tricks of a sophist.
The flame asks Dante if he truly possesses the 'coin' of faith within his own soul.
Dante identifies the Holy Spirit as the source of the profound certainty and joy that constitutes his faith.
Dante argues that the truth of divine revelation is proven by the transformative works that follow it.
The flame challenges Dante, questioning how the effects of a work can prove the nature of the creator.
Dante points to the miraculous growth of Christianity as proof of Christ's divine mission.
The entire heavenly court erupts in a synchronized hymn of praise: 'Deo laudamus'.
St. Peter guides Dante deeper into the celestial layers toward the ultimate heights.
St. Peter encourages Dante to articulate the source and nature of his belief.
Dante prepares himself to testify to the contents of his faith before the Apostle.
Dante proclaims his belief in the one eternal God and the mystery of the Holy Trinity.
Dante describes how divine truth acts as a spark within the human mind, expanding into a bright flame.
St. Peter embraces Dante three times in joyful benediction upon hearing his testimony.
